commands.author: Takhoffman
commands.repair_reason: automerge enabled; PR is behind or has merge conflicts and needs a cloud rebase repair before automerge

target.title: fix(installer): avoid before with npm release-age configs
target.body:
Fixes #84743

## Summary
- detect raw npmrc `min-release-age` settings in the Unix hosted installer scripts before considering npm's computed `before` value
- keep the freshness bypass on `--min-release-age=0` when raw config already contains `min-release-age`, avoiding npm's mutually exclusive `--before` combination
- add shell-level regression coverage for both `install.sh` and `install-cli.sh`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The Unix installer no longer emits `--before=<timestamp>` when the user's raw npmrc contains `min-release-age`, even if `npm config get before` would return a computed cutoff.

**Real environment tested:** Local macOS shell environment in `/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-84743`, using the real OpenClaw `scripts/install.sh` function and real npm against a temporary npmrc plus temporary npm global prefix. The live proof installed a temporary local package into the temporary prefix so the machine's global npm install location was not mutated.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a temporary npmrc containing `min-release-age=7`, a temporary npm global prefix, and a tiny local package with a bin entry. Ran real npm with the conflicting old-style command shape: `npm --userconfig <temp-npmrc> --before=2026-05-21T00:20:45.000Z config get before`. Then sourced patched OpenClaw `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1`, `NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG=<temp-npmrc>`, and `NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=<temp-prefix>`, and ran `run_npm_global_install <temp-package> <temp-log>`. Supplemental checks: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts test/scripts/install-cli.test.ts test/scripts/install-ps1.test.ts src/infra/npm-install-env.test.ts`; `bash -n scripts/install.sh scripts/install-cli.sh`; `git diff --check`.

**Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output from the real npm/OpenClaw installer proof:

```text
npm_conflict_status=1
npm_conflict_first_line=Exit prior to config file resolving
installer_status=0
last_npm_install_cmd=env -u NPM_CONFIG_BEFORE -u npm_config_before -u NPM_CONFIG_MIN_RELEASE_AGE -u npm_config_min_release_age -u npm_config_min-release-age SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1 npm --loglevel error --silent --no-fund --no-audit --min-release-age=0 install -g /var/folders/sl/5dkd3zq12dv65j6jx57zq1hc0000gn/T/openclaw-84743-proof-ChuZVt/pkg
installed_bin_exists=yes
install_log_tail=
```

Supplemental regression evidence: Vitest result was 4 test files passed, 59 tests passed. Shell syntax check and `git diff --check` completed with no output.

**Observed result after fix:** For raw npmrc release-age policies, the real `install.sh` path preserved the non-conflicting `--min-release-age=0` bypass instead of switching to `--before=<timestamp>`. npm accepted the install command and installed the temporary package into the temporary prefix, while the old conflicting `--before` command shape reproduced npm's `Exit prior to config file resolving` failure.

**What was not tested:** I did not run the hosted `curl | bash` installer or install the published `openclaw` package globally because that would be slower and more invasive than the temporary-prefix proof. I did not change PowerShell installer behavior; the existing `install-ps1` tests were included in the acceptance run.

## Attribution
If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution for Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> or include:

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>
target.author: turbotheturtle
